cachedTaxSuggestions.title: Maximize 401(k) Contributions
cachedTaxSuggestions.description: Given the significant salary income from Amazon, prioritize contributing the maximum annual limit ($23,000 for 2024) to a traditional 401(k) to reduce your taxable income dollar-for-dollar.

cachedTaxSuggestions.title: Fund a Health Savings Account (HSA)
cachedTaxSuggestions.description: If enrolled in a high-deductible health plan, contribute to an HSA. Contributions are 100% tax-deductible (up to $8,300 for families in 2024), reducing your overall tax liability while providing tax-free growth for medical costs.

cachedTaxSuggestions.title: Deduct State Sales Tax
cachedTaxSuggestions.description: As a resident of Bellevue, WA, you pay no state income tax. Ensure you track and deduct state and local sales taxes on Schedule A of your federal return if your itemized deductions exceed the standard deduction.

cachedTaxSuggestions.title: Execute a Backdoor Roth IRA
cachedTaxSuggestions.description: Your household income level likely exceeds the threshold for direct Roth IRA contributions. Use the backdoor method—making a non-deductible contribution to a traditional IRA and converting it—to gain tax-free growth.

cachedTaxSuggestions.title: Bundle Charitable Contributions
cachedTaxSuggestions.description: Since you are currently renting and may not have high mortgage interest deductions, consider 'bundling' several years of charitable donations into a single tax year to surpass the standard deduction threshold and maximize tax savings.
